---
title: ""
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-band}
::: {.hero-eyebrow}
18 formats · one pipeline · MIT open source
:::

::: {.hero-wordmark}
livefigures
:::

::: {.hero-thesis}
The source is the figure.
:::

::: {.hero-sub}
Reference an `.excalidraw`, `.vl.json`, or `.dot` file — or write four
lines of diagram inline — and `quarto render` draws it. Version-controlled,
never stale, and editable by **you and your AI agents** alike.
:::

::: {.hero-ctas}
[Get started →](install.qmd){.cta-primary}
[See the gallery](gallery/index.qmd){.cta-ink}
:::

::: {.hero-install}
`quarto add seandavi/quarto-livefigures`
:::
:::

::: {.grid .hero-demo}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-5}
::: {.demo-source}
::: {.demo-filename}
figures/flow.noml
:::
```default
#direction: right
[edit source] -> [quarto render]
[quarto render] -> [figure]
```
:::
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-2 .demo-arrow}
renders to ⟶
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-5}
```{.nomnoml #fig-hero fig-cap="A figure whose source you just read — authored inline in this page." width=100%}
#direction: right
[edit source] -> [quarto render]
[quarto render] -> [figure]
```
:::
:::

No exported SVGs to maintain. No generated files in version control.
Captions, cross-references (like @fig-hero), sizing, subfigures, and
lightbox all behave exactly like native Quarto figures — because they
*are* native Quarto figures by the time Quarto sees them.

## One idea, four tools

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-6 .g-col-lg-3 .deliverable-card}
### Extension
Figures from source files or fenced blocks, rendered inside
`quarto render`. HTML, PDF, slides.

[Get started →](install.qmd){.card-go}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-6 .g-col-lg-3 .deliverable-card}
### MCP server
Your agent writes a figure, calls `render`, and **sees the image** —
before it lands in your document.

[Set up MCP →](mcp.qmd){.card-go}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-6 .g-col-lg-3 .deliverable-card}
### CLI
`npx livefigures` — render and validate any figure source from the
shell or CI.

[CLI commands →](cli.qmd){.card-go}
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-sm-6 .g-col-lg-3 .deliverable-card}
### Agent skill
One Markdown briefing that makes any coding agent fluent in all
18 formats.

[Brief your agent →](agents.qmd){.card-go}
:::
:::

## The loop

Change the source, render, done — the figure can never go stale, because
there is nothing to forget to regenerate:

![](demo.gif){fig-alt="Editing a nomnoml source file and re-rendering: the figure updates to match" .demo-gif}

And hand-drawn sources are real, editable scenes — this one is
[`arch.excalidraw`](https://github.com/seandavi/quarto-livefigures/blob/main/site/gallery/figures/arch.excalidraw),
which you can open at [excalidraw.com](https://excalidraw.com/) (@fig-hero2):

![The file is the figure](gallery/figures/arch.excalidraw){#fig-hero2 width=55%}

## 18 formats, one pipeline {.format-badges}

**Local & offline:** `.excalidraw` `.vl.json` `.vg.json` `.dot` `.gv`
`.dbml` `.noml` `.wavedrom` `.bytefield`

**Via kroki:** `.puml` `.d2` `.c4` `.structurizr` `.erd` `.ditaa`
`.pikchr` `.svgbob` `.tikz`

Every format was chosen for **agent fluency** — text and JSON sources
LLMs author reliably. See the [gallery](gallery/index.qmd) for all of
them, live, or [Formats](formats.qmd) for choosing one.

## Doesn't Quarto already do this?

Quarto ships excellent built-in support for Mermaid and Graphviz — as
*executable code cells*. livefigures is complementary, and different in
kind:

| | Native ` ```{mermaid} ` / ` ```{dot} ` | livefigures |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Formats | 2 | 18 — incl. Excalidraw, Vega-Lite, PlantUML, WaveDrom … |
| Source | code cells only | **files** (`![](arch.excalidraw)`) or fenced blocks |
| Editable-tool formats | — | Excalidraw scenes edited in a real editor |
| PDF output | browser-dependent | bundled deterministic rasterizer, correct fonts |
| Caching | per-render | content-addressed, instant warm rebuilds |

If Mermaid in a code cell covers your needs, use it! livefigures is for
everything it doesn't cover.

The same goes for the community extensions —
[pandoc-ext/diagram](https://github.com/pandoc-ext/diagram) (code cells
via locally installed tools) and the
[two](https://github.com/resepemb/quarto-kroki)
[quarto-kroki](https://github.com/fermarsan/quarto-kroki) filters (code
cells via a kroki server). livefigures differs in kind:
**file-referenced sources** with native image syntax, **bundled
renderers** (nothing to install, no network for local formats),
deterministic PDF, content-addressed caching, and the agent tooling
([skill](agents.qmd), [MCP](mcp.qmd), [CLI](cli.qmd)).

## Why

Diagrams-as-code tools occupy an awkward middle ground: editable
sources, static outputs, manual exports that go stale, and both checked
into git. livefigures removes the export step — it's the
literate-programming idea [@knuth1984literate] applied to the one part
of a document that still lives outside it. And now that AI agents write
and revise documents alongside us, a figure that exists as fifteen lines
of reviewable text is a figure [an agent can maintain](agents.qmd).

::: {.route-line}
Whatever brings you here, there's a short path: **writing a paper or
site** → [Get started](install.qmd) · **browsing what it can draw** →
[Gallery](gallery/index.qmd) · **setting up your agent** →
[Agent skill](agents.qmd), [MCP server](mcp.qmd), or the
[CLI](cli.qmd).
:::
